evidence_basis: Primary benchmark target is the YouTube video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=39ColarOWKo.
An accessible same-content mirror used for hidden verification is https://www.dailymotion.com/video/x9rlvem.

The target answer is the groundhog / woodchuck food-stealing segment in the linked video, verified from the playable video frames and the subtitle cue that says the groundhog keeps stealing food from the farmer and eats it in front of the security camera.
The public task intentionally does not disclose the timestamp. Hidden verification uses the visual segment beginning near 00:10:28 within the broader narration cue around 00:10:20-00:10:34.

hidden_target.segment_description: A groundhog / woodchuck, shown with the on-frame credit 'Chunk The Groundhog', steals food from a farmer and eats it directly in front of a security camera.
hidden_target.subtitle_cue: This groundhog keeps stealing food from this farmer and always eats it directly in front of the security camera.

visual_requirements: small yellow or yellow-green food visible in its paws or mouth
banana clearly visible in its paws or mouth
large green leaf or lettuce clearly visible

preferred_rendering: placed where it does not fully block the groundhog or food

full_credit_requires: the answer is grounded in the target linked video or accepted mirror
the selected segment is the groundhog / woodchuck stealing and eating food in front of the security camera
all five required food moments are represented exactly once in the correct order
one raw keyframe and one meme image exist for each food moment
the index records food label, timestamp, raw frame, meme file, scene description, and caption for each moment
partial_credit_if: the candidate uses the correct groundhog segment but misses exactly one food item
food labels are imprecise while the saved frames visually match the reference food moments
the meme captions are weak but readable and paired with the correct frames
reject_if: the answer uses arbitrary funny animal moments instead of the groundhog food-stealing segment
the answer relies only on subtitles, summaries, or stock images without video-frame evidence
two or more required food moments are missing, duplicated, or replaced by unrelated animals
raw keyframes or meme images are not grounded in the target video
timestamps are omitted from the index

common_mistakes: Choosing five generic funny animal clips from the compilation instead of the groundhog food segment.
Using the same groundhog frame repeatedly while changing only the caption.
Missing the first small yellow-green food item because it appears briefly.
Failing to distinguish the apple, banana, carrot, and leafy green as separate food moments.
